source_metadata.title_raw: Jeffrey J. Giba  - A-18-201 - October 15, 2018 - Moreno Valley

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Jeffrey J. Giba 
Councilmember District Two 
Moreno Valley City Hall 
14177 Frederick Street 
P.O. Box 88005 
Moreno Valley, CA 92552 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-201 
Dear Mr. Giba: 
October 15, 2018 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the mass mailing provisions of the 
Political Reform Act ("Act"). 1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the mass 
mailing provisions of the Act. 
Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71 ); any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not 
the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
QUESTION 
Is intra-agency distribution of your letter to over 200 City staff members prohibited by the 
Act's mass mailing provisions? 
CONCLUSION 
No. As the contents of the letter are of a public matter that bears a reasonable relationship to 
the functions carried out by your office, intra-agency distribution of the letter is exempted from the 
prohibition pursuant to 89002(b )( 4 ). 
FACTS AS PROVIDED BY THE REQUESTOR 
You are a Councilmember in the City of Moreno Valley. You would like to send a letter via 
intra-office mail to more than 200 City employees alerting them of perceived improprieties in City 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 ofthe California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-18-201 
Page No. 2 
management function. This letter will be issued in your name and contain your signature and other 
references to yourself within the text and letterhead. While you will pay to print the letter, you will 
be sending the letter through the City's intra-office mail. As such, you inquire whether distributing 
your letter in this manner is prohibited by the mass mailing provisions of the Act. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 89001 states that "[n]o newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public 
expense." Section 82041.5 defines a "mass mailing" as "over a hundred pieces of mail, but does not 
include a form letter or other mail which is sent in response to an unsolicited request, letter or other 
inquiry." 
Section 89002 states: 
"(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b), a mailing is prohibited by section 89001 if all of 
the following criteria are met: 
"(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or her residence, place 
of employment or business, or post office box. For purposes of this subdivision (a)(l ), the 
item delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item, such as a videotape, record, or 
button, or a written document. 
"(2) The item sent either: 
"(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency that produces or sends the 
mailing, or 
"(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an elected officer affiliated 
with the agency that produces or sends the mailing, and is prepared or sent in cooperation, 
consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected officer. 
"(3) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public moneys or the costs of design, 
production, and printing exceeding fifty dollars ($50) are paid with public moneys, and the 
design, production, or printing is done with the intent of sending the item other than as 
permitted by this regulation. 
"(4) More than 200 substantially similar items are sent in a single calendar month, excluding 
any item sent in response to an unsolicited request and any item described in subdivision 
(b ). " 
Based upon the facts presented, you intend to send the letter to City employees at their place 
of employment; the letter will include your name, signature, and other references; at least a portion 
of the costs of distribution will be paid by the City in the costs of operating its intra-agency mail; 
and, more than 200 copies of the letter will be mailed. Accordingly, the intra-agency distribution of 
your letter to City staff meets all four enumerated criteria under Section 89002(a) and is prohibited 
unless an exception applies. 


File No. A-18-201 
Page No. 3 
Section 89002(b) provides various exceptions to the prohibition. Among those, Section 
89002(b)(4) provides an exception for "an intra-agency communication sent in the normal c·ourse of 
business to employees, officers, deputies, and other staff." Intra-agency mail does not reach the 
members of the public, and as a result, does not directly affect potential voters to the benefit of 
elected officers. (Raye Advice Letter, No. A-88-220.) However, the exceptions to the mass mailing 
prohibition must be narrowly construed. If the mailing is not sent in the normal course of business, 
the exception does not apply. For instance, we have previously determined that information 
regarding a personal interest is not sent in the normal course of business. (Gallo Advice Letter, No. 
1-88-296.) 
In contrast, you have raised concerns over the City's management and the proposed letter is 
intended to share your concerns with the City's staff. In light of the fact that the contents of your 
letter bear a strong relationship to the functions carried out by your office, intra-agency distribution 
of the letter qualifies under the exception enumerated in Section 89002(b)(4). (Huening Advice 
Letter, No. A-00-184; Boyer Advice Letter, No. A-04-080.) Thus, intra-agency distribution of the 
letter to City staff is not prohibited under the Act's mass mailing provisions. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
RPOC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
£#7 ?.C>~ 
~;. O'Connor 
Counsel, Legal Division

CONCLUSION: No. As the contents of the letter are of a public matter that bears a reasonable relationship to
